days.content_direction: Tiffany speaks directly to the multi-passionate entrepreneur who mistakes activity for momentum. She calls out the pattern of collecting ideas instead of committing to one and challenges them to stop researching and start deciding.
days.hook: You have seventeen tabs open and zero revenue from any of them. That is not a strategy problem. That is a decision problem.
days.shot_list: Tiffany on camera, direct eye contact, intentional pauses for emphasis, clean background, no props
days.thumbnail_hook: 17 tabs. Zero revenue. This is a decision problem.
days.title: Your Business Does Not Need More Ideas. It Needs a Decision.
days.voiceover_topic: Seventeen tabs open and zero revenue from any of them

days.content_direction: Pull Tiffany's documented language about approaching Scripture with fresh eyes and pair it with a brief caption inviting the audience to examine what they think they already know about God's character.
days.pillar: Spiritual Development and Gods Math Teaching
days.quote: You have to go to the Bible like a newborn - as if you've never learned anything about love, about acceptance, about forgiveness.
days.title: You Have to Go to the Bible Like a Newborn

days.carousel_outline: Slide 1: Cover - '5 Things I Had to Unlearn as a Wife Before I Could Build as a Partner' | Slide 2: I had to unlearn that love means never needing anything - covenant requires communication, not silence | Slide 3: I had to unlearn keeping score - scorekeeping is a survival tool, not a partnership tool | Slide 4: I had to unlearn making every disagreement a referendum on the whole marriage - not every conflict is a crisis | Slide 5: I had to unlearn performing strength - my husband needed my honesty more than my composure | Slide 6: I had to unlearn building alone - God designed us to build together, not in parallel | Slide 7: Call to reflection - What are you still carrying into your home that was never meant to live there?
days.content_direction: Tiffany shares honest, grounded reflections on the mindset shifts she had to make in marriage, connecting personal testimony with the legacy-building narrative that runs through her brand. Each slide names one unlearned habit and what replaced it.
days.hook: Nobody teaches you that the habits you bring into your marriage are the same ones that will either build your household or burn it down.
days.title: 5 Things I Had to Unlearn as a Wife Before I Could Build as a Partner

days.content_direction: Tiffany teaches the God's Math principle using a real, tangible moment where the numbers did not make sense but obedience moved things anyway. She is authoritative and personal, not preachy.
days.hook: You keep trying to figure out how it is going to work. God is not asking you to figure it out. He is asking you to obey.
days.pillar: Spiritual Development and Gods Math Teaching
days.shot_list: Tiffany on camera, warm lighting, conversational and direct, slight lean-in for the teaching moment
days.thumbnail_hook: God's math will never add up to your calculator.
days.title: God's Math Does Not Work Like Your Math
days.voiceover_topic: Obedience moves what your calculator never could

days.content_direction: Use Tiffany's documented language about the real signal that you are ready to hire, paired with a caption that reframes hiring readiness as a stewardship decision, not just a financial one.
days.quote: When you're ready to hire: when not hiring them is either costing you money or time you're no longer willing to give up.
days.title: When Not Hiring Is Costing You More Than Hiring Would

days.content_direction: Tiffany leans into her relatable mom humor while delivering a genuine spiritual truth about worry and trust. The tone is lighthearted but the landing is real and convicted.
days.hook: My child looked at me and said 'Mommy, why do you keep worrying if God already said He got it?' I have been in ministry for years. A six-year-old just ministered to me.
days.shot_list: Tiffany on camera, laughing at herself, storytelling energy, natural and unpolished feel to match the humor of the moment
days.thumbnail_hook: My kid just preached me under the table.
days.title: My Kid Just Preached a Whole Sermon Without Knowing It
days.voiceover_topic: My six-year-old preached and I was not ready

days.carousel_outline: Slide 1: Cover - '3 Signs You Have Been Worshipping a Version of God Someone Else Built' | Slide 2: Sign 1 - You brace for punishment every time things go wrong instead of running toward Him | Slide 3: Sign 2 - You perform for His approval instead of resting in His acceptance | Slide 4: Sign 3 - You believe He is present in the good seasons but unavailable in the hard ones | Slide 5: Where did that come from? Most of us attach the personality of God to the people who first taught us about love - and those people were human and broken | Slide 6: The invitation - Go back to Scripture like a newborn. Let God define Himself. | Slide 7: Closing - Psalm 103:8 - The Lord is compassionate and gracious, slow to anger, abounding in love.
days.content_direction: Tiffany walks the audience through three markers that reveal a distorted image of God rooted in human experience, not biblical truth. Each slide is honest, warm, and points toward healing rather than shame.
days.hook: The God you pray to might be shaped more by your childhood than by Scripture. That is not a condemnation. That is an invitation.
days.pillar: Spiritual Development and Gods Math Teaching
days.title: 3 Signs You Have Been Worshipping a Version of God Someone Else Built

days.content_direction: Tiffany uses the brain dump analogy from the knowledge base to illustrate what premature or unstructured delegation looks like in real life, then pivots to what should be in place before anyone new walks through the door. Direct, practical, and convicting without shame.
days.hook: You hired them because you said you were drowning. But you handed them a pile with no instructions and wondered why nothing got better.
days.shot_list: Tiffany on camera, builds energy through the story, slows down for the practical pivot, clear and direct delivery
days.thumbnail_hook: Hiring without a process is just organized chaos with a paycheck.
days.title: Hiring Someone Without a Process Is Like Throwing Files on Their Desk and Saying Fix It
days.voiceover_topic: What you hand your hire tells them everything about your business

days.content_direction: Use Tiffany's documented voice on the two hiring personality types to craft a quote post that names the real motivation behind premature hiring decisions, paired with a caption that draws the line between exhaustion-driven decisions and readiness-driven ones.
days.quote: You're going to have months where you're paying for someone and they don't have anything organized to do. And now you're for sure wasting your money.
days.title: Most Hirers Are Not Ready. They Are Just Exhausted.

days.content_direction: Tiffany shares a personal testimony moment from her home where a real conversation shifted the household financial posture from survival to legacy. The tone is intimate, grounded, and invites the audience into the behind-the-scenes of what building for generations actually looks like in daily life.
days.hook: We stopped asking what we could afford. We started asking what we were building toward. Those are two completely different questions.
days.shot_list: Tiffany on camera, warm and reflective tone, storytelling pace, personal and unscripted-feeling delivery
days.thumbnail_hook: We stopped asking what we could afford and everything changed.
days.title: The Conversation at Our Dinner Table That Changed How We Spend Money
days.voiceover_topic: The dinner table question that changed how we move with money

days.carousel_outline: Slide 1: Cover - 'Before You Post That Hiring Ad: What Needs to Be in Place First' | Slide 2: A written job description that includes outcomes, not just tasks - they need to know what winning looks like | Slide 3: A weekly audit process - how will you check in without micromanaging everything they touch | Slide 4: Documented SOPs for at least the top 3 tasks they will own - do not hand them a blank page and call it delegation | Slide 5: A clear answer to this question - what does this role free me up to do? If you cannot answer that, you are not ready | Slide 6: Three to six months of payroll in your operating account - not in your head, in your account | Slide 7: A defined onboarding window - they need time to learn, not a deadline to perform miracles | Slide 8: Closing - Build the structure first. Then invite someone into it. That is not slowing down. That is stewardship.
days.content_direction: Tiffany delivers a practical, no-fluff pre-hire checklist carousel that speaks to the overwhelmed entrepreneur who is ready to delegate but has not built the structure for someone to step into. Each slide is actionable and grounded in her Don't Scale Dysfunction teaching.
days.hook: The hire is not the hard part. What you hand them on day one is the hard part. Here is what needs to exist before they show up.
days.title: Before You Post That Hiring Ad: A Checklist for What Should Already Be in Place

days.content_direction: Tiffany speaks into the fear of readiness that keeps people on the sideline of what God has called them to, drawing from community testimony and her own story without fabricating quotes. She is the compassionate big sister calling them forward with conviction.
days.hook: Every woman I know who has walked into something great said the same thing beforehand. I do not think I am ready. God keeps sending people before they feel qualified. That is the pattern.
days.shot_list: Tiffany on camera, eye contact, builds from calm to conviction, ends with a direct challenge to the audience
days.thumbnail_hook: God is not waiting until you feel ready.
days.title: She Said She Was Not Ready. God Said Otherwise.
days.voiceover_topic: God keeps sending people before they feel qualified

days.content_direction: Use Tiffany's documented language about adopting God's identity from other people's behavior to create a quote post that lands the core Detox Your View of God teaching in a single, shareable moment. Caption invites reflection, not guilt.
days.pillar: Spiritual Development and Gods Math Teaching
days.quote: Most of us adopt the identity that other people have given us, and we attach that to the personality of God, and it's just not true.
days.title: What You Attach to God Tells You More About Your Past Than His Character

days.content_direction: Tiffany leans into the humor of the entrepreneur Sunday spiral while landing softly on the real need for intentional rest as an act of trust, not laziness. Funny, relatable, and grounded in who she actually is.
days.hook: Sunday afternoon hits different when you own a business. The Sabbath is right there. The email is also right there. Lord help me.
days.shot_list: Tiffany on camera, relaxed setting, playful and self-aware energy, natural laugh moments, ends on a genuine note about rest
days.thumbnail_hook: Sabbath is right there. The emails are also right there.
days.title: Nobody Told Me Entrepreneurship Would Feel Like This on a Sunday
days.voiceover_topic: Sunday energy when you own a business and love Jesus

designer1.notion_copy:
